title: Longest Concatenated Palindromic Substring with K Swaps or Reversals

statement.markdown: Write a function that returns the length of the longest concatenated palindromic substring that can be formed from a given string $s$ with at most $k$ swaps or reversals. A swap operation changes a character in $s$ to any other character, and a reversal operation reverses the substring of $s$ between two indices. If there's no palindromic substring that can be concatenated, return $0$. Note that a substring is considered to be empty.
statement.outputFormat: length of the longest concatenated palindromic substring
statement.notes: You can swap characters in $s$, add characters in front of $s$, or remove characters from the start of $s$. You can also choose to do nothing to the original string $s$.

examples.explanation: One good solution is to take the string "abcdea". This is a palindrome of length 6.
One good solution is to take the string "abcdefggfedcba". This is a palindrome of length 8.
One good solution is to take the string "abcddcba". This is a palindrome of length 8.

hints.text: Use a dynamic programming approach to track the longest concatenated palindromic substring for each prefix of the input string.

editorial.title: Longest Concatenated Palindromic Substring with K Swaps or Reversals
editorial.intuition: The key idea is to use a dynamic programming table to track the longest concatenated palindromic substring for each prefix of the input string. We can then use this table to compute the final answer.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a dynamic programming approach to track the longest concatenated palindromic substring for each prefix of the input string.

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: int solve(string s, int k) { int n = s.size(); if (n == 0 || k == 0) return 0; int dp[n][n]; int maxVal = 0; for (int i = n - 1; i >= 0; --i) { dp[i][i] = 1; for (int j = i + 1; j < n; ++j) { if (s[i] == s[j]) dp[i][j] = dp[i + 1][j - 1] + 2; else dp[i][j] = max(dp[i + 1][j], dp[i][j - 1]); } maxVal = max(maxVal, dp[i][j]); } return maxVal; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public boolean solve(String s, int k) { int n = s.length(); if (n == 0 || k == 0) return false; int[][] dp = new int[n][n]; int maxVal = 0; for (int i = n - 1; i >= 0; i--) { dp[i][i] = 1; for (int j = i + 1; j < n; j++) { if (s.charAt(i) == s.charAt(j)) dp[i][j] = dp[i + 1][j - 1] + 2; else dp[i][j] = Math.max(dp[i + 1][j], dp[i][j - 1]); } maxVal = Math.max(maxVal, dp[i][j]); } return maxVal; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, s: str, k: int) -> int: n = len(s); if n == 0 or k == 0: return 0; dp = [[0 for x in range(n+1)] for y in range(n+1)]; max_val = 0; for i in range(n-1, -1, -1): dp[i][i] = 1; for j in range(i+1, n): if s[i] == s[j]: dp[i][j] = dp[i+1][j-1] + 2; else: dp[i][j] = max(dp[i+1][j], dp[i][j-1]); max_val = max(max_val, dp[i][j]); return max_val;
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function solve(s, k) { let n = s.length; if (n == 0 || k == 0) return 0; let dp = []; let maxVal = 0; for (let i = n - 1; i >= 0; i--) { dp[i] = new Array(n + 1).fill(0); dp[i][i] = 1; for (let j = i + 1; j < n; j++) { if (s[i] == s[j]) dp[i][j] = dp[i + 1][j - 1] + 2; else dp[i][j] = Math.max(dp[i + 1][j], dp[i][j - 1]); } maxVal = Math.max(maxVal, dp[i][j]); } return maxVal; }

solutions.cpp: class Solution { public: int solve(string s, int k) { int n = s.size(); if (n == 0 || k == 0) return 0; int dp[n][n]; int maxVal = 0; for (int i = n - 1; i >= 0; --i) { dp[i][i] = 1; for (int j = i + 1; j < n; ++j) { if (s[i] == s[j]) dp[i][j] = dp[i + 1][j - 1] + 2; else dp[i][j] = max(dp[i + 1][j], dp[i][j - 1]); } maxVal = max(maxVal, dp[i][j]); } return maxVal; } };
solutions.java: public class Solution { public boolean solve(String s, int k) { int n = s.length(); if (n == 0 || k == 0) return false; int[][] dp = new int[n][n]; int maxVal = 0; for (int i = n - 1; i >= 0; i--) { dp[i][i] = 1; for (int j = i + 1; j < n; j++) { if (s.charAt(i) == s.charAt(j)) dp[i][j] = dp[i + 1][j - 1] + 2; else dp[i][j] = Math.max(dp[i + 1][j], dp[i][j - 1]); } maxVal = Math.max(maxVal, dp[i][j]); } return maxVal > 0; } }
solutions.python: class Solution: def solve(self, s: str, k: int) -> int: n = len(s); if n == 0 or k == 0: return 0; dp = [[0 for x in range(n+1)] for y in range(n+1)]; max_val = 0; for i in range(n-1, -1, -1): dp[i][i] = 1; for j in range(i+1, n): if s[i] == s[j]: dp[i][j] = dp[i+1][j-1] + 2; else: dp[i][j] = max(dp[i+1][j], dp[i][j-1]); max_val = max(max_val, dp[i][j]); return max_val;
solutions.javascript: function solve(s, k) { let n = s.length; if (n == 0 || k == 0) return 0; let dp = []; let maxVal = 0; for (let i = n - 1; i >= 0; i--) { dp[i] = new Array(n + 1).fill(0); dp[i][i] = 1; for (let j = i + 1; j < n; j++) { if (s[i] == s[j]) dp[i][j] = dp[i + 1][j - 1] + 2; else dp[i][j] = Math.max(dp[i + 1][j], dp[i][j - 1]); } maxVal = Math.max(maxVal, dp[i][j]); } return maxVal; }
